decisions.reasoning: High Horsepower provides the best damage output against Iron Hands (48.5-57.2%), putting it in range for a 2HKO while being more effective than Glacial Lance due to the type matchup.
Iron Hands is at 4% HP, meaning any move will secure the KO. Glacial Lance is the most reliable option to finish the opponent and remove the threat immediately.

Calyrex-Ice is at 57% and Incineroar threatens a 2HKO with Flare Blitz while outspeeding. Switching to my own Incineroar allows me to Intimidate the opponent and safely pivot or check their physical attackers.
Since both Incineroars resist each other's primary STAB moves and are in a speed tie, Parting Shot is the best way to maintain momentum, force a switch, and debuff the opponent's incoming Pokemon.
Since we are in a speed tie and the opponent's Incineroar is likely to stay in to trade chip damage, Knock Off is the best play to remove their item and gain information while maintaining momentum.

Flutter Mane is guaranteed to be OHKO'd by Flare Blitz and cannot secure a KO on Incineroar this turn. Switching to my own Incineroar provides a safe defensive pivot that resists the predicted Flare Blitz.
Since both Incineroars resist each other's primary STABs, this is a neutral stalemate. Parting Shot allows me to pivot out safely, debuff the opponent, and bring in a teammate to gain momentum.

Incineroar is threatened with an OHKO by Close Combat and a 2HKO by Surging Strikes, and it is slower than Urshifu-RS. Switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to Fighting-type moves and forces the opponent to respect the speed advantage.
I outspeed Urshifu-Rapid-Strike and Moonblast is a guaranteed OHKO, removing a major threat immediately.
Flutter Mane is fainted; I must send in a resist to Urshifu-Rapid-Strike's Surging Strikes. Amoonguss resists Water-type moves and can pivot or threaten with Spore/Giga Drain.

Calyrex-Ice is significantly slower and taking heavy chip damage from Surging Strikes. Switching to Amoonguss provides a resistance to Water-type moves and allows me to scout the opponent's next move while preserving Calyrex-Ice for later.

Incineroar has already fainted, so I must bring in a resist to Urshifu-Rapid's Surging Strikes. Amoonguss provides a crucial Water-type resistance and can threaten to put the opponent to sleep or force a switch.

Calyrex-Ice is significantly slower and cannot KO Urshifu, while Surging Strikes will likely KO Calyrex-Ice given its current 57% HP. Switching to Amoonguss provides a resistance to Water-type moves and allows for potential redirection or status support.

Calyrex-Ice is at 20% and will be KO'd by Surging Strikes this turn. Switching to Amoonguss provides a resistance to Water-type moves and allows me to scout the opponent's next move while preserving my remaining offensive threats.
